item.title: FDA Baby Formula Safety
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA has updated its approach to baby formula safety testing, focusing on contaminants such as heavy metals and pathogens. The agency's new guidelines aim to strengthen oversight and ensure product safety following past recalls and consumer concerns. The initiative does not involve specific drugs but highlights broader regulatory efforts in infant nutrition.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: USDA Brain Drain
item.description:
• Summary: Food safety experts warn that the USDA is losing critical scientific staff, potentially compromising food safety oversight.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Alternative Medicine Placebo
item.description:
• Summary: The article explores the role of placebo effects in alternative medicine, questioning the cost and time spent on treatments lacking proven efficacy beyond placebo.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Medical School Nutrition
item.description:
• Summary: This podcast discusses the importance of nutrition education in medical schools and its role in preventive medicine.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
